title: Two-site Bose-Hubbard hopping and Schrödinger cat states
authors: Madeline Berezowski, Artur Sowa, Jonas Fransson
abstract: The Bose-Hubbard Hamiltonian can be simplified to have only two lattice sites, in which case the system being described is referred to as a dimer. Due to its structure, the hopping term of the dimer Hamiltonian enjoys invariance in a family of subspaces indexed by a whole number $k$, each subspace corresponding to a system of only $k$ particles. We have invented an inductive argument using the bosonic canonical commutation relations to find the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of the dimer hopping Hamiltonian in its $k$-particle subspaces. In particular, this Hamiltonian, when restricted to one of the $k$-particle subspaces, is exactly the spin projection operator along the $x$-axis, where the number of particles $k$ in the dimer system yields the projection matrix for spin quantum number $s=k/2$. Thus, a new method for computing the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of the $x$-axis spin projector has been unearthed. We use the explicit construction to study the dynamics of coherent states induced by the square of the dimer hopping hamiltonian. We find that it generates Schrödinger cat states in the two-site setting.
summary: This paper provides an exact analytical solution for the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of the hopping term in a two-site Bose-Hubbard model. It shows that the square of this hopping Hamiltonian can periodically transform coherent states into Schrödinger cat states. Additionally, it uncovers a new method for computing the properties of the spin x-projection operator.
main_problem: The paper aims to characterize the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of the hopping term in a two-site Bose-Hubbard model and study the generation of Schrödinger cat states.
main_result: The authors demonstrate that the square of the dimer hopping Hamiltonian induces periodic evolution of coherent states into macroscopically distinguishable Schrödinger cat states and provide a new method for diagonalizing the spin x-projection operator.
method: An inductive argument using bosonic canonical commutation relations is used to derive eigenvalues and eigenvectors, alongside an analysis of the dynamical evolution under the H^2 operator.
model_or_system: A Bose-Hubbard dimer consisting of two lattice sites containing k spinless bosons, where the Hamiltonian focus is on the hopping term.
key_observables: Energy eigenvalues, probability distribution of energy measurements, and the formation of Schrödinger cat states.
important_parameters: Number of particles (k), hopping amplitude (t), on-site interaction strength (U), and chemical potential (mu).
main_assumptions: The derivation assumes the validity of Bose-Hubbard-type commutation relations for transformed operators and focuses specifically on the dynamics induced by the square of the Hamiltonian.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates the relationship between the number of particles k and the resulting system properties.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the dimer model, presents an inductive proof for the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of the hopping Hamiltonian, establishes the equivalence to the spin x-projection operator, and concludes with the dynamical analysis of coherent state evolution into cat states.
why_interesting: It provides an analytical framework for generating non-classical, macroscopically distinguishable superpositions (cat states) using a simple two-site model, which is highly relevant to quantum state engineering and many-body dynamics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
